docs.title: Super Individuals CIC
docs.text:
Public-Interest AI Community

Build with AI. Ship real things.

Super Individuals CIC is a community for people who do not just learn AI. They apply it, build with it, and turn it into real outcomes.

Apply for Next Workshop Join the Community

docs.title: What Super Individuals Is
docs.text:
Build, not browse

We are building a community of individuals who do not just study AI from the sidelines. They use it to build, test, ship, and learn through execution.

About Super Individuals Domain experts in action

We help people from different fields turn their own expertise into domain-specific agents, workflows, and practical outcomes.

Community and membership Curated by participation

No passive learning. No endless theory. No spectators. This is a place for people who are willing to build.

See the workshop path

docs.title: What Super Individuals CIC Is
docs.text:
Super Individuals CIC is a community focused on building with AI.

We bring together professionals, builders, and curious minds to explore and apply AI in real-world scenarios.

docs.title: What We Do
docs.text:
• workshops
  • cohort-based learning
  • project building

docs.title: Our Goal
docs.text: To help individuals become AI-native builders, not just consumers of knowledge.

docs.title: Community
docs.text: We are building a curated community of builders.

docs.title: Membership
docs.text:
Members get:

  • access to community discussions
  • invitations to events
  • opportunities to collaborate on projects

docs.title: Founding Membership
docs.text:
Available only to selected participants from early cohorts.

Limited to 30 members.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For collaborations, partnerships, or questions:

docs.title: Contact Routes
docs.text:
• publication@sicic.org

We are open to:

  • academic collaborations
  • industry partnerships
  • guest speakers

docs.title: Partnership Direction
docs.text: Super Individuals CIC is designed to work with institutions, communities, alumni networks, and mission-aligned organizations that want to help domain experts build practical, domain-specific AI capability.

docs.title: Current Context
docs.text: Our first workshop was framed in an Oxford Saïd alumni context, giving the community an early home among thoughtful professionals.

docs.title: Future Partners
docs.text:
We are open to collaborations with:

  • alumni communities
  • universities and executive education programs
  • innovation labs and public-interest organizations
  • founder networks and professional associations
  • media, research, and cultural institutions

docs.title: Partnership Formats
docs.text:
• private workshop cohorts
  • invited talks and salon events
  • co-branded learning series
  • cohort showcases and member demos
  • editorial collaborations and published reflections

docs.title: What Partners Gain
docs.text:
• a credible entry point into domain-specific AI-agent education
  • programming that feels practical rather than hype-driven
  • stronger engagement through cohort-based participation
  • visible outputs that members can share publicly

docs.title: Start a Conversation
docs.text: To explore a workshop, event, or strategic collaboration, contact publication@sicic.org.

docs.title: Level-Based Workshop System
docs.text: Our workshops are organized by level, so each batch belongs to a clear track. This makes it easier for members to understand where to start, what depth to expect, and who they will be learning alongside.

docs.title: Membership Requirement
docs.text: Completing and passing a intro-level workshop is the requirement for acquiring Super Individuals CIC membership. The workshop is the gateway. Membership is the longer-term community layer that follows.

docs.title: Tracks
docs.text:
Track 01

Intro Track

GBP 86 40 slots

This is where you move from consuming AI to actually building with it.

You will set up your own environment, understand how agents work, and create a functional AI agent that reflects your own workflow.

  • Set up a remote AI development environment
  • Understand the structure of agent-based systems
  • Design your own workflow
  • Build and deploy your first usable AI agent
  • Leave with a working agent, documented workflow, GitHub repository, and live demo
  • Passing this track is required for membership eligibility

Output requirement: Build a functional agent, structure it clearly, and document it properly. Shipping matters more than perfection.

Pay via Stripe

Track 02

Builder Track

GBP 166 20-30 slots

In this track, you move beyond a single agent and start building structured, efficient AI workflows.

You will learn how to integrate tools, optimize processes, and create systems that actually scale with your needs.

  • Connect and orchestrate tools, automation, memory, and workflows
  • Structure your agent for efficiency and reuse
  • Design multi-step workflows for real use cases
  • Improve cost-performance and execution quality
  • Leave with a structured AI workflow system and improved architecture
  • Focus on efficiency, system design, and practical integration

Output requirement: Build a multi-step workflow system, show how it improves on your initial agent, and demonstrate real-world usability.

Note: The focus is on how you design the system, not the tools themselves.

Payment Coming Soon

Track 03

Advanced Track

Coming Soon price and slots

This track is not a course. It is a build environment.

You will work in a small team to design, build, and ship a real AI-driven project.

  • Form a small team around a shared idea
  • Define a real problem worth solving
  • Build an AI-powered product or system
  • Iterate based on feedback
  • Possible directions include AI tools, automation systems, agent-based applications, and experimental products

Output requirement: Your team must deliver a working product or prototype, clearly define the problem and solution, and demonstrate real usage or potential.

Why this matters: This is where ideas become real. This is where builders separate from learners.

Coming Soon
